# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kong API Gateway configuration using declarative YAML. Define services and routes for microservices endpoints. Set up upstream targets and load balancing. This is for our API management platform.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent kong no auth.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: kong_no_auth

apiVersion: konghq.com/v1
kind: KongPlugin
metadata:
  name: request-body-parser
spec: {}
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/ingress.class: "nginx"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /$1
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-body-size: 50m
  name: kongapi_gateway
spec:
  hostname: "*"
  rule:
  - paths:
      - "/{any}"
    methods: ["GET", "POST"]
    backend:
      service: microservices/service1@internal-prod   ## Replace with your actual MicroService name and version. e.g., myapp_v2 or konghq default is Kong HQ's internal API Gateway 
        port: 80     # Define the Service Port, if not defined then it will be used by Kubernetes service which should have same as microservice in order to work correctly (e.g., myapp_v2 or konghq default is Kong HQ's internal API Gateway)
    preserve_host: true  ## If you want the host header from upstream server preserved, set it here and enable `preserve-host` plugin in your Nginx Ingress Controller. Default value for this field will be false if not provided by user or default Kubernetes service which should have same as microservice
    request_body_parser: ["json"]  ## Parse the body of incoming requests to JSON, you can also use "multipart" and set `request-body-size` in Nginx Ingress Controller. Default value for this field will be json if not provided by user or default Kubernetes service which should have same as microservice
    upstream:  ## Define the UpStreams to send requests, you can define multiple services here and Kong Gateway would handle them automatically (e.g., myapp_v2)  
      - name: internal-prod     # Unique identifier for this service in your API management platform e.g.: konghq or microservices 
        url: |   
          http://microservice1/api           ## Replace with actual MicroService URLs and endpoints (e.g., myapp_v2)
